conjecture_title: Informal Conjecture (fractional treewidth-fragility of sublinear-separator classes)
paper_title: Approximation metatheorems for classes with bounded expansion
summary: Dvořák conjectures in arXiv:2103.08698 that all classes with sublinear separators are fractionally treewidth-fragile. The paper itself establishes the property for hereditary classes with sublinear separators and bounded maximum degree, for all proper minor-closed classes, and for several geometric graph classes, but the full conjecture without the bounded-degree restriction remains open. No follow-up paper proving or disproving the general statement was found across multiple targeted searches.
search_queries: fractionally treewidth-fragile sublinear separators Dvořák conjecture
notes: The conjecture is stated informally in the paper: the bounded-degree case is proved therein, as are proper minor-closed classes and geometric classes. The gap is the general sublinear-separator case without bounded degree. Related work (arXiv:2208.10074 on product structure for strongly-sublinear-separator classes) does not address fractional treewidth-fragility. No resolution found in the literature as of 2026-05-15.
